primary_legislation.all: Work at Height Regulations 2005; Health and Safety at Work Act 1974

notes: Steps are identical to school MOD-010. Responsible person updated to Practice Manager. Common WAH tasks in GP practices: changing ceiling light bulbs and fluorescent tubes, reaching high shelving in store rooms and dispensary, cleaning high-level surfaces and windows, external maintenance tasks.

logbooks.title: Monthly Ladder and Steps Pre-Use Inspection Log
logbooks.frequency_detail: Monthly formal record. Staff must also visually check equipment immediately before each use.

steps.description: Locate all WAH equipment in the practice: step ladders, hop-ups, platform steps, extension ladders (if any).

For each item inspect: stiles (no bends, cracks or damage), rungs/treads (all present, firm, no damage, non-slip surface intact), feet (rubber feet present and intact, anti-slip pads in good condition), locking mechanism (for stepladders: confirm spreader bar locks open fully), labels and ratings legible.
steps.photo_guidance: Photograph any damaged rung, foot, or locking mechanism.

steps.description: Any WAH equipment with a defect must be taken out of service immediately, tagged 'Do Not Use', and either repaired or disposed of. Do not return to service until defect is rectified.
Confirm all WAH equipment is stored correctly (flat or hanging, not leant against a wall unsecured).
Record: date, equipment reference, condition (pass/fail per item), defects found, action taken, inspector name.

logbooks.title: Annual WAH Equipment Formal Inspection
logbooks.frequency_detail: Once per year. Annual inspection to be carried out by a competent person (may be the Practice Manager or Site Manager if trained) per PUWER / WAH Regulations.

steps.description: Compile a register of all WAH equipment on site. Confirm each item is marked with a unique reference.

Carry out a detailed formal inspection of each item: check all structural components, joints, hinges, fixings, non-slip surfaces, and markings. Compare against manufacturer guidance for inspection criteria.
Record the condition of each item, with a recommendation: continue in service / monitor / remove from service.
Any item failing the formal inspection must be removed from service, tagged, and either repaired by a competent person or replaced.
Record: date, all equipment reference numbers, inspection findings per item, any removed from service, inspector name and competence, next formal inspection due.

logbooks.title: Annual Work at Height Risk Assessment Review

steps.description: Identify all tasks in the practice that require work at height: changing light fittings, accessing high shelving (dispensary, store room), external gutter or roof access, window cleaning above ground floor.

Apply the WAH hierarchy for each task: avoid (can the task be redesigned?), collective prevention (scaffolding, mobile elevated platform), personal prevention (harness), and as a last resort, mitigation (nets, air bags). For GP practices, step ladders and hop-ups are typically sufficient for internal tasks.

Confirm that fragile surfaces (roofs, ceiling panels, any glazed surfaces overhead) are clearly identified and that a prohibition is in place for staff accessing these without specialist contractor involvement.
Confirm all staff who use WAH equipment are trained in safe use (buddy system, three points of contact, correct angle for leaning ladders).
Record: date, tasks identified, controls reviewed and confirmed, training confirmed, any changes to assessment, next review date.
